name: Sitecore Experience Platform DXP API
what_it_does: Enterprise Digital Experience Platform (DXP) with REST and GraphQL API — enabling large organizations to manage content, personalize experiences using xDB behavioral data, run A/B tests, automate email campaigns, and track customer journeys across web, mobile, and digital channels, with cloud-native XM Cloud for composable headless architecture.
use_cases: Delivering personalized content via Sitecore XM Cloud GraphQL API for headless Next.js/React frontend
Managing content items and media via Sitecore Content Management API for content operations
Querying xDB visitor profiles and behavioral data via Sitecore API for personalization decisions
Integrating Sitecore with Salesforce, SAP, or custom CRMs via Sitecore Connect for data sync
Triggering Sitecore email campaigns and marketing automation via API for cross-channel orchestration
not_for: Small and mid-market companies (Sitecore pricing and complexity are enterprise-only)
Developer-only headless CMS without marketing features (use Contentful for simpler headless)
Rapid deployment (Sitecore implementation takes 6-18+ months for enterprise)
best_when: A large enterprise marketing team needs the market's most mature personalization engine combined with content management — especially in pharma, financial services, retail, and media where behavioral personalization, compliance, and enterprise scale are critical.
avoid_when: You're not enterprise-scale, don't have a Sitecore implementation partner, need rapid time-to-value, or primarily need headless CMS without personalization complexity.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow for system integrations. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped with role-based access. Webhook for content publish and workflow events. GraphQL Edge for fast content delivery. Management REST API for content operations. XM Cloud: SaaS with managed infrastructure. Sitecore Connect (MuleSoft-based) for enterprise integration. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. .NET SDK (XP) and JavaScript/TypeScript SDK (XM Cloud).

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; enterprise pricing only
pricing.paid_tiers: XM Cloud from $100,000+/year; XP (on-premises) license from $50,000+/year; contact sales
pricing.notes: Copenhagen company (Denmark). Founded 1998. Raised $1.2B from Silver Lake Partners (2021) at $9.2B valuation. 5,200+ employees. Serving 35,000+ digital properties across 100+ countries. Sitecore XP (Experience Platform) is on-premises/managed cloud. Sitecore XM Cloud is SaaS headless (launched 2022). Composable DXP strategy (CDP, Personalize, Send, OrderCloud, Search as separate products). Customers include American Express, L'Oréal, Volvo, Michelin. Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for DXP. 1,500+ implementation partner network.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise rate limits per contract; Edge GraphQL has high throughput

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL Edge + REST Management API. OAuth2 auth. Extensive enterprise documentation. Webhook for content events. XM Cloud has modern SaaS API; XP has legacy .NET-heavy API. Implementation partner typically required before API integration. Edge GraphQL is high performance for content delivery. Management API has rate limits that require careful implementation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. Item reads idempotent by ID. Edge GraphQL queries idempotent. Management mutations require item ID for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Implementation partner (SI) typically required — 6-18 month implementation before API integration
XP (on-premises) and XM Cloud (SaaS) have different API patterns — verify which is deployed
Composable DXP strategy means multiple products (CDP, Personalize, Send) each with separate APIs
Enterprise pricing ($100K+/year) makes evaluation expensive without commitment
xDB behavioral data (XP) requires GDPR compliance configuration — significant setup

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS compliant. Multi-region data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. Silver Lake ($9.2B valuation) with enterprise security standards. xDB behavioral/personalization data is highly privacy-sensitive — strict GDPR compliance required. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. HIPAA BAA for healthcare. PCI-DSS for e-commerce. Composable DXP means multiple security boundaries across products — unified identity management essential.
